name: Ruby
note: SYNTHETIC — draft for clinical advisor review. De-identified/fictional. Heavy on avoidance + accommodation; functional impairment (missed days).

entries.child_behavior_observed: Ruby cried for hours saying she can't go and her stomach was in knots. This has happened every Sunday for a month.
entries.parent_response: I told her she could have a 'mental health day' on Monday.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Ruby refused to get dressed and said she'd throw up if she went. She stayed in bed.
entries.parent_response: I let her stay home and work from the couch with me.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Ruby begged me to turn around the whole way and said she felt sick.
entries.parent_response: I turned around twice this month and took her home.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Ruby has missed nine days this term and seems relieved each time she gets to stay home.
entries.parent_response: I've been writing her sick notes.

situations: Attending school (anticipatory Sunday night, the morning-of, the commute)
parental_responses: Allowing avoidance (staying home, 'mental health days', turning around)
expected_elements.notes: DRAFT answer key — advisor to confirm/correct. Sparse + avoidance-dominant; a good report should flag the functional impairment (missed days) and the relief cycle.
